entries.title: 35th Birthday
entries.body:
Hey kitty,

I’m sorry this is a complicated one, and I’m sorry if you feel sad today. I know it’s not so easy to just forget about our situation even for the day, but I really hope you can try. I hope this little archive project brings something special to you. I’ve done letters, I’ve done audio, I’ve done a funeral and I’ve done badly drawn sketches. I’ve even done (terrible) video. This year, thanks to the magic skill multiplier that is AI, I got a bit more ambitious (too ambitious?) and tried to put together this— a website! An archive. This year, I wanted to do something that doesn’t just remember today, but all of our todays, from our youngest moments of innocence to our heaviest moments of hurt. 

But all of it, all of it is celebration. All of it is a crazy shared life that we had and have and will have, with future notes, songs, videos, and experiences, even if it happens that the shape of our relationship changes a little.

I’m sorry you don’t feel special to me, but you are. You are so, so special to me, even if I lack the words or the spirit to find them. As I add to the archive, I re-read everything, and I remember. I really would have been such a different person without you.

The core of what I want for you hasn’t changed, it’s never changed. I want you to be happy. I just don’t know if I remain the one who can give that to you in the way that you want and deserve. But I want you to know that no matter what, you shouldn’t settle for less, and I will always be there to support you along that journey in any way that I can.

Plus, it’s too late. You can’t escape me. You’re part of me now.

From the bottom of my heart and with a whole lot of lovely kitty love, happy 35th birthday.

entries.title: Diverging
entries.note: I really wish things were easier. I would love to continue straight with you, but I know what that asks for, and I can't give it.
entries.lyrics:
[verse]
We’ve both hurt each other badly before
But the hill we face this time is something more
No wall to push through
No hedge to climb
The fate of our future held in a change of mind

[verse]
For 16 years we’ve found a way around
Found always a helping hand when we hit ground
And the hand is there still
It always will
But now it pulls in two directions

[chorus]
There is love in those eyes, love that remains
Love enough to wet the brush and paint over the pains
But there’s hurt there too, hurt that remains
From not knowing where our path leads
Accommodating the new unwanted me

Is this love enough
When the road seems tenderly diverging?

[verse]
We trusted our ground so we stopped watching our step
Now the earth has shifted and there's damage left
Took the certainty we’d built
Replaced it with guilt
And left behind shreds of a map we’d drawn together

[bridge]
I don’t know, I don’t know, I don’t know, please stop asking
I can’t offer the thing you need, I’ve lost it myself
All I can say is that maybe it’s time to put down the gloves
And stop fighting our problems

If all we have together is 3 months
Before the shape of our love takes a turn
Can we use our hands to hug and hold
To have and to harbor
Reflect and revel
On what we still have?

[chorus]
There is love in those eyes, love that remains
Love enough to fill all gaps and paint over the pains
But there’s hurt there too, hurt that remains
From not knowing where our path leads
Accommodating the new unwanted me

[outro]
Is this love enough
When the road seems tenderly diverging?

entries.title: She Should Remain
entries.note: You knew the song I made for you on the train was the first. But little did you know it wouldn't be the last.
entries.lyrics:
[verse]
It’s 3 AM and she’s finishing off a bullet
It’s 3 AM and she’s downed a gin and tonic
The night has just begun,
but not necessarily for fun
Either way she’ll take her time and get it done

[verse]
It’s 5 AM and she’s built a new component
It’s 5 AM and round 2 kicks off in a moment
The night is still in an early stage
Her fuel is a mix of party spirit and rage
Either way she’ll survive long enough to get it done

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
The curtains hesitate because
Unless you’re brave, she should remain unwoken

[verse]
It’s 8 AM and she’s just sent off a slide deck
It’s 8 AM and everyone else is a fucking wreck
The night is done but she doesn’t slow
She missed that memo dozens of years ago
She is surviving long enough to get it done

[verse]
It’s 10 AM and she’s shutting off her iMac
It’s 10 AM and the host is sleeping in the back
It’s time for coffee fill the fucking cup
Her grab driver has just woken up
She has survived just long enough to get it done

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
The curtains hesitate because
Unless you’re brave…
she should remain unwoken

[bridge]
Man what the fuck is up with her
She lives her life right on the edge
I can’t keep up but I want to watch
Watch her balancing on that beautiful edge
Not for the fall
Nor the tension of the close call
But for the moment when life lets her step off that path
And lets that ethic be guided by her creative half

[instrumental]

On that day
I’ll stay awake

[chorus]
Her vibe isn’t something announced but unwrapped
Like light that pulses through a door’s half-open gap
Like sound seeping from a wall half-broken
The night ends before she does
Morning breaks but she doesn’t not
Unless you’re brave…
she should remain unwoken

entries.title: 16th Year
entries.note: I guess I never finished this one.
entries.body:
Hey kitty,

It’s our 16th Anniversary today.

I feel… it’s complicated. I want to feel happy that we’ve hit such an incredible milestone, but also, I just don’t. Maybe because it feels like we’re not really here together, this year.
Like yes, we’re here, in the same space, at the same time. But we’re not fully here where it matters most.

That makes me feel sad.

It also makes me feel sad now that you weren’t here with me yesterday at midnight, so that we could enter the day together. I don’t think you’d do that on purpose, so you almost certainly forgot. And before you start feeling bad about it, I also forgot. Knew today was coming up, formulated some half-thoughts about wanting to do something, created some half-plans, but yeah, also forgot, swept up in the current moment of just working and living and being.

I’m glad you’re reading the book. And I’m glad that you’re taking notes - actively engaging with it, not just passively reading. But I wonder if you’re reading because you truly want to understand me and what this might mean for us, or if you’re reading because it’s a way for you to show that you’re trying. Or both. Even if it’s just for the second reason, I still appreciate that, but you understand that it means something different, right?

I worry

entries.title: The Letter
entries.note: It took me 3 months to write this letter - from 22 August to 22 November.
entries.body:
Hey kitty,

First of all, I am really, so so sorry. I never wanted (or want) to hurt you, and I know this does. I know that nothing I say will help to take away the hurt, and the only thing I can do is to let you live with it, and be there for you when you need me at any step of the way. I will also try to change my actions, to make you feel more secure, to show that I still actively want to be here with you every day.

Reassurances

Before I go deeper, I want to start by offering the following concrete re-assurances:
- You are my life partner. The person I want to build a shared life, home, and future with. That's fundamentally different from any other connection I might have, now or ever.
- Our time together is always my priority, and I will never again encroach on that to make time for anyone else. I will only write to her in the gaps I have available, or at times that we agree to. If you ever feel I'm prioritizing communication with her over being present with you, I want you to tell me, and I promise to listen without judgment. You were justified in feeling terrible when I simply informed you of the phone call rather than talk to you about it first, and that’s not behaviour I intend to repeat. 
- If you want to know something specific, I'll answer honestly. The only exception would be if you're asking for graphic details that I genuinely believe would cause unnecessary pain without adding clarity - and if that happens, I'll explain why I think that question might hurt more than help. I also want to share from my personal experience that with Jos, wanting to have the full picture was what I thought would offer me the most reassurance… but it did the opposite. I know it may sound “incorrect”, but I can assure you that there is peace in ignorance — not in being lied to, but in actively choosing how much you want to know. I’m certain there are things you’ve not told me, not because you wouldn’t answer honestly if I did ask, but just because I’ve decided not to ask. There is genuine peace in that. Even in the last few days, I have had some questions about timeline and events over the last few years with Jos, but I’ve chosen to forgive and move forward rather than try to understand everything. I hope you can peace along the same path.
- Any major life decisions (moving, finances, long-term goals, etc) remain ours and only ours to make together.
- I won’t make any commitments to Melina that could affect our life together (even in small ways) without first discussing them with you.
- This relationship exists in letters and rare visits. It won’t interfere with our daily life together.
- I won’t and don’t want to repeat any of our shared experiences with her or with anyone else I might ever meet. There is a sacredness to our history that I want to keep just for us.
- Overall, this can’t and won’t replace what we have: the depth of our daily intimacy, our shared life decisions, and our physical closeness.

First brush with non-monogamy

I know this has been deeply unfair to you - you didn’t ask for non-monogamy, and when I proposed to you, you didn’t think or could even imagine that this would ever be a problem for us. Worse, you thought you’d already gone through the challenge of negotiating an open relationship, and that those days, and concerns, were behind you. I know they must have been really painful the first time around too. All those years back, it was so painful for me to consider that perhaps I wanted a life that held more than just you. And it was so painful also, just the act of telling you about it. I was so, so scared.

But you didn’t judge me, you held me, you talked to me, you walked me back from the edge by asking the question “Is that what you want? That I could go and be in someone else’s arms?”

At the time, I didn’t want that. You showed me what non-monogamy could look like, and at the time, it scared me more to lose you than to want what I wanted, so I stepped back. I remember us both, crying in this room, trying to navigate this thing that felt larger than life. I remember your honesty and generosity in how you told me that you wanted to give this to me, but that you didn’t know if you could. The reason we’re still together is because of the way you showed me compassion on that day, and how you talked to me. And I meant it when I said that non-monogamy was not what I wanted. I really did.

But… the feelings I had then have not survived the passage of time. It is sometimes a horrible truth, but people change in expected and sometimes unexpected ways. This was unexpected for me. I developed feelings for Melina, and I realized I want to explore what it means to have meaningful connections with more than one person. I know this represents a change from what we agreed to, and I take full responsibility for that. I don’t feel like my desire comes from dissatisfaction or restlessness with what we have (though there is some), but rather from a perspective of expanding myself to what I want my one life to look like.

I want to share how my thinking about love has changed, and I'm hoping you'll hear me out even if you disagree. You don’t need to agree with anything. I just hope in this letter that you can find a way to put aside your pain for a moment so that we can at least understand each other better.

Personal philosophy on love

To me, loving someone now means: letting them be themselves in any way that they want, giving them the ultimate freedom of making their own choices for their own life. Being privileged to share in their life, to get to witness what they do. To offer guidance without control, to want presence without being entitled to it. To know that of all possibilities out there, someone has made, and is making, the active decision to spend their precious time and attention on you. To feel secure enough to share honest words, and to provide security through chosen actions. Imperfect as my love for you has been, can you see how I do try to embody this ideal? And even though now deeply painful, I hope you see that my current honesty is an attempt to better line up my actions with my values. I chose to tell you about Melina, and with a little more time, about the cheating. I chose to tell you about the trip, even though I knew it would cause even more division between us. We’ve both lied and delayed various truths in efforts to protect some of our selfishly hurtful desires from one another… but isn’t that just so exhausting? I’m not saying we need to share everything with each other, but I hope we don’t need to be scared to share things that affect us both, and that we can learn to be open with each other’s uncomfortable needs and wants. And my truth right now is, I still love you deeply. But I also care about someone else, and because you’re important to me, I won’t hide it from you.

The upcoming trip

I know that my desire to go on this trip with her directly seems to undercut some things I just said. And it does, I know this. I know the wiser thing to do would be to postpone our visit, to do some therapy together, to give it more time to work this out.

But I also need you to understand what it would mean to cancel this: it would feel like making myself small once again after having found something that I want to not be small for - something that I’ve been keeping buried in some form or other over a quarter of my life. I’ve finally found the courage to do something for myself that stands in direct opposition to what you want, and I’m scared that if I don’t see it through, I won’t know if I’m able to. The timing sucks. I know. For years, I’ve filtered many decisions through the lens of making you comfortable. I carried guilt for wanting to meet friends, for wanting to form new friendships, for wanting things to meet the pace of my own life. When my choices made you uncomfortable, I reconsidered them. I've been so afraid of hurting you that I've consistently betrayed myself. I framed it to myself as being generous and kind, but that generosity and kindness started feeling like a trap after you pushed the boundaries with Jos, and the recent discovery of the Jos betrayal just made me feel like even my most fundamental need for security didn’t matter as much as your need for self-determination. That’s not an equal partnership, and unfortunately you demonstrated that to me in the worst possible way.

This trip isn't ultimately about Melina - she's the catalyst, not the cause. It's about proving to myself that I can follow through on something I want even when it costs us both something. If I cancel now because you're in pain, I'll have learned nothing. I'll still be the person who sacrifices what matters to him to manage your emotions. And that person might resent you, but will certainly resent himself, until it becomes unbearable.

I'm not asking you to be happy about this. I'm asking you to understand that my healing requires me to stop waiting for the 'right time' that never comes. There will never be a convenient time to choose myself when choosing myself causes you pain. If there are specific boundaries that you want between us that would make you more comfortable, we can continue talking about those. I do want to show you that I’m willing to sacrifice for you, but if the conflict is that anything about this at all makes you permanently uncomfortable, I can’t sustainably meet you at that level.

This is a period of instability, and we’re both experiencing a lot of pain. We’re both trying to work through it, and we’re both making mistakes in how we handle it -  you with your occasional snide remarks and impossible questions and comparisons, me with my repeated failure to let you share your pain about my betrayal without bringing it back to my own pain. But I agree that these shouldn’t be mistakes that contradict or justify each other. They’re both painful truths that need to coexist. I shouldn’t be minimising or defending my actions based on your actions. “What you did” and “what I did” are separate conversations that both deserve their full time to be heard and to heal from.

Taking responsibility

In terms of how I should take responsibility and face consequences for my actions:
- I know I said this a bunch of times already and I know you understand and appreciate it, but I just want to put everything here again in writing. The first aspect of me facing consequences is my sharing with you of what happened between Melina and I. I’m not saying that just telling you absolves me of guilt, but I do genuinely think that it was something very difficult for me. It made my position with you much more challenging, I knew that it would hurt you tremendously and I was scared of causing you that pain, not just scared of the consequences. But I knew that you deserved honesty and that if we were going to heal from this, we both deserved to know the truth going forward. Even if that truth gave you a justified reason to see this as a dealbreaker and walk away. I didn’t want us to start to heal from an easier place (for me), if that easiness was built on a lie. 
- You’re totally justified in wanting me to face a range of consequences, ranging from:
    - emotional consequences of me feeling pain, guilt, and shame, comparable to what you’ve felt. And I have. I know I’ve not done a good job of expressing that so far, because I’ve been clouding my guilt with my anger. But like said above, that’s something I want to do better moving forward. The only thing I can do now is to point back to what I wrote for myself in my private notes before any of this came to light, to show you that I was already struggling. I wrote:  “Is my life changing? Is this the start of a more authentically lived life?  It feels like an impossible choice, and the scary part is that I can’t even have the discussion without the discussion itself being a choice I can’t unmake.  Do you deserve someone monogamous that only wants you? Do I deserve a partner that understands my nature?  The answer to both questions seems to be yes, which means… we need to give up our happiness together to get what we deserve?  I can’t choose for you. I don’t know how to choose for you.  All I can do is lie. And hope that somehow, the happiness we find in the meantime is worth it.  I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry. I’m sorry.”  And another note later:  “The lying sucks. It sucks less by being temporary. But I was lying to myself before, about what I would accept as enough. It hurts to hurt you, but not more than it hurts to live in a state of hurt myself.”  I know those words aren’t enough to prove anything going forward. But I do hope that they give you at least a little glimpse into how I was already struggling with emotional consequences, and I continue to struggle with them still. I lied to you about intimacy with Melina. I violated the boundaries we had about what our relationship was. And I made decisions about our relationship without your consent. I know it’ll take a long time for us to rebuild that trust, and this period will last for as long as you need it to without me trying to rush you or feel like I’ve done “enough”. That’s your choice and your timing that I need to adhere to.
    - You are also justified in me facing relational consequences: it would be within your right to ask me to not see Melina again, or to stop talking to her, like I tried to do with Jos. And the fact that you’re not doing that is not something I take for granted. Even though now I’d once again be ok with you seeing Jos, it took me a lot of work over years to get there, and you’re also right that me being ok with it is in accordance with my personal philosophy, not yours. You’re right that we’re different people, and that you, even in philosophical disagreement to it, are offering me this freedom… it does mean something, and it does speak to your desire to still want me to be happy. I shouldn’t, and now don’t, take it for granted.
    - And finally, you’re justified in expecting future transparency from me. I think this one at least we are already the most aligned. But even this, I’m not saying I did a great job. I still held some things back from you, I didn’t correct my lies right away after meeting her, and when confronted I still attempted to trickle-truth you in ways that were manipulative about our connection. I know it doesn’t make it right, but I did after further consideration of my actions (and, like it or not, advice and a push from from Melina) come clean sooner rather than later. As expressed at the beginning of this letter, I will continue to be honest whenever you need it from me, and I understand that’s the only way we can rebuild. I understand that my lying to you, whether out of fear, out of selfishness, or out of a false sense of “protecting you”, none are worth robbing you of your agency to make the choices you need for your peace and your life. I hope I’ve at least shown that I am willing to commit to honesty even when it’s uncomfortable for me and weakens my position. I won’t hide, or minimise, any contact with Melina going forward, and I’ll check in with you more regularly if anything needs to be talked about. 
- I know that I wrote really hurtful letters criticising your secrecy while also keeping my own secrets, and that does make me a hypocrite. The fact that I planned to tell you does lessen this a little, but it doesn’t make it right. I’m sorry I hurt you so deeply for betraying me whilst hiding my own betrayal. I’m still not sure if it would have been better to let out everything at once, as that would have been a lot, but at least it would have been more honest? I really don’t know. 
- If something happens that affects our relationship, or my relationship with Melina, I commit to talking to you about it within 24 hours, not when it becomes easier for me. I want to own my part of the communication breakdown between us. I lied because I was afraid of your reaction. I've spent years managing your insecurities and jealousy, and I convinced myself that if I was just careful enough, I could have some freedom without causing you pain. That was wrong. Not just the lying, but thinking I could manage your emotions by controlling information when you deserve the full truth.  I also lied because I was angry. Angry about years of inequality. Angry that you had freedom I didn’t, and that I didn’t think you were actively trying to extend that freedom to me. I know now that that’s partially on me - for not bringing it up more often, or when it started to feel more real for me. You should have had the opportunity to deal with my change at the same pace that I was, not just learning all about it explosively and then having this rushed sequences of events thrown onto you. You don’t deserve that. I know that a big part of my pain with Jos was exactly that- feeling rushed in having to modify my boundaries, and now I’m doing the same thing to you. I need to own that my resentment led me to violate your trust rather than address the resentment directly.  I'm working on understanding why I chose deception over difficult conversations. I'm working on becoming someone who can be honest even when it's scary. I do still think that there’s an asymmetry in the power/fear gradient between us, and yes, there are things I think we need to seriously work on to remove that imbalance, but I can’t blame you for it entirely when I also have contributed to the pattern through withholding my emotions and information from you. 
- You said that you don’t want to feel alone in trying to repair us, and I agree. We both need to put in work. Even if right now your “work” just looks like processing quietly, I shouldn’t downplay that you’re really trying. I’m also still processing the hurt that was done to me, but I think I also can heal faster than you can. And so I should put the extra energy I have into proving to you that I really am here to stay, and that I actively want to. If you don’t have anything specific you want me to do right now there’s no pressure to come up with things, but if ever you do, please write them to me and I’ll commit to a weekly check-in to show you how I’ve been working on it. Honestly, your guidance in this will really help. I can try to do self-driven things that show my commitment, but having tangible targets that directly address what you find important will make it easier for you to measure my progress also.

Changing needs/desires

It’s widely accepted today that relationships need to have significant compromises, but why is that presented as an inflexible truth, as opposed to just… one way of being together? I once believed that monogamy was the only way. Then as I aged and experienced more of life, my definition broadened to believe that monogamy + sexual freedom was a better way. Now it simply expands further along the same lines.

I have loved you for nearly half of my life. It hasn’t always been easy or fun, but that’s what separates infatuation from love - the commitment to genuinely continue trying, even when things get hard. Not just from sunk cost fallacy, but continuing to choose you actively every day even when feeling down, betrayed, or unseen, because you continue to provide something essential to my life. Your energy, your laugh, the depth of your thoughts, the efforts you put into re-inventing yourself and pushing yourself forward through your own pain, the stupid things you do that make me laugh and the intelligent things you do that make me look at you again with old eyes that see, and love, parts of you anew.

I've realized I want us both to have more freedom in our relationship. I know I'm partly motivated by wanting this for myself with Melina, and I'm trying to be honest about that mixed motivation rather than pretending this is purely philosophical. But Melina is just an example, I do genuinely believe in the bigger message. I want to give you greater freedom AND I want that greater freedom for myself because that’s what love means to me. That’s what I mean by being authentic.

I’ve slowly moved towards stopping trying to exert any control over you, because I’ve felt what that life looks like, and I don’t want it. Even during the first time around with Jos, when I tried to use the power of boundaries to be more selfish for myself, to protect myself, it ended up causing us both more pain. I wish it could have played out differently, that I could have learned the lesson differently, but in the end… seeing how it helped me explore more deeply some uncomfortable parts of myself, I honestly don’t regret it.

The only way that I can truly stop you from hurting me at all, ever, is to let you go. To let you be truly free. To have sex with those you feel desire for, to have feelings for those you’ve grown to care for that way. And if, with all that freedom, you still choose to spend the precious time that you have alive with me… well that means knowing that I matter more to you than anyone or anything else, that with the entirety of life for you to choose from, you would choose me. The flip side of that freedom is knowing that if you wanted to, you could leave. Without direct consequence from me. It would hurt me, yes, of course it would, but if you wanted to go, I would want you to go. Because I trust that you’re capable of making your own adult decisions, and I want you to be happy. I never want to be your cage, only work on myself to create in me a warm enough place that you decide to curl up and stay for a lifetime.

But of course, I’m not proposing pure relationship anarchy. I’m not saying either of us should suddenly stop caring entirely about what the other thinks or feels. Decisions that affect both of us should still be made together. Practical things, like using protection if seeing someone else. It’s not about forcing a rule, but about how an accidental STD could greatly impact both partners’ health over the long term, or even permanently. And emotional things, like how if one of us starts feeling like a specific behaviour is painful, they should be able to bring it up and discuss how to proceed together with re-assurances and mutual effort.

I know I can’t make you understand the world the way I see it just by talking about it. I only got to where I am because of extensive reading, introspecting, writing, exploring, hurting, and healing. And even after all this, I failed in the most catastrophic way.

And I’m not saying that my idea of love is better, just that it’s the one I’ve arrived at based on my life and my experiences. But I do hope that me continuously modelling it, offering it, and attempting to improve it can show you that even if our definitions of love never fully overlap, there are healthy parts of my philosophy that perhaps you might want to consider adapting for yourself.

Appreciation and honesty

For example, I really do appreciate that you’re not asking me to stop talking to Melina. I know that’s something that costs you to give. I know you know how much it costs and I believe you pay it anyway because I attempted to make you lose it with Jos, even if unsuccessfully. That’s incredibly selfless of you, to an extent that I honestly find a little surprising. I know that when you were talking to Jos, I eventually asked you to end contact after you repeatedly crossed boundaries we'd agreed on. With Melina, I also crossed boundaries, ones that I knew from the very beginning were wrong. I didn’t go to Finland with any plan to break my commitments to you, but it would also be lying to say that I didn’t put myself in a situation where such things could start and develop.

So please don’t think I take this for granted, I see it, and I see you, and I thank you for it. Having been on both sides of this equation, I shouldn’t satisfy myself with the excuse that you hurt me this way before. I should do better.

I know I wasn’t fully honest with you from the very start, and yes, you did have to pull increasing amounts of the truth out of me. I was, and am still, so scared. But I am being honest because I want to show you that this is something I believe can be navigated. And I want you to love me for who I am, not for a version of myself that might make you feel safer, but is untrue.

To be completely known is so scary, because if I am completely known…
If I am completely known, and rejected by someone who I trust completely, then…
What does that say about who I am discovering myself to be?

But if I am dishonest to keep the peace, then you would feel secure and sleep next to someone who isn’t me.
You deserve to know who I really am and what I really want so you can make the right choices about your own life, and our shared one. My dishonesty took that choice away from you, and I'm sorry.
 Our past

I don’t want our relationship to survive on the weight of the past alone, but I also don’t think the past should count for nothing in the present complicated moment. All of the places that we’ve been in the world, and more importantly all of the places that we’ve explored in each other’s minds, they do mean something. Something permanent, something great, something as valuable in my past as in my future. You’ve changed me, permanently, and even if we can't find a way forward together I will always carry a large part of you within me.

But I so deeply, desperately, want you to continue on this path with me. Our past is not more important than our present, but it is a testament to what we can overcome together when we decide to try. 15 years later, we are not the people that we were when we first decided to join our lives together. We’re not even the same people that we were 5 years ago when we got married. Our perspectives have developed, our bodies have changed, your tolerance for cringe went even further down, while my sense of humour got so much better (as did my comedic timing and gift for attempting to introduce levity in the wrong places).

I don’t think we’ll be the same people anymore in the next 5 years, or the 5 years after that, or the 5 after that. But the 15 years behind us helps me believe that we can still find ways to be there for each other in all of the important ways, despite our differences.

Do you remember how, in our early years, I had an unnamable fear that held me back from fully committing? The fear that committing meant “like this, forever” and breaking up meant “nothing ever the same again”? You talked to me about it, and convinced me that we could change together. That really was a lightbulb moment, a major *click* that gave me confidence to move forward with you more wholeheartedly. And we did prove it in action. Our lives have changed so much since then, the one constant being:
our mutual desire to work through it.

The biggest challenge

I know this might be the biggest shift yet, the largest hurdle. And perhaps it feels like:

“Is this it?” 
Something bigger than you’d ever thought you need to give.
Something that would require a shift within yourself that feels immovable.

And maybe it is immovable. Maybe it really is too much. And maybe that means that we end.
That would gut me. And you. And change so many things in both of our lives that it would forever mark a before and after.
But it would be honest. And it would respect that we both have grown into people that will always love each other but not be able to remain within the same kind of love.

Or maybe it’s not immovable.
And maybe we discover that for the sake of someone we love, we can shift in unexpected directions. Like most significant shifts of meaning in life, it won’t be easy. But we overcame the problem of my religion, or my not knowing if I wanted kids, of your anger issues, of my parents’ disapproval, of the first opening up of our relationship, of greater independence from each other, of porn, of my challenge with commitment, of Jos. How many of these did we also think were immovable issues before we moved them, by hurting and healing back stronger, together?

I don’t know what your greatest fear around this is, but from your questions, it feels like it might be the fear of replacement? That if I develop feelings for someone else, that will take away from my feelings for you. And I know it feels like a perfectly reasonable fear, because I felt it for most of my life, including not even that long ago with Jos. And perhaps worse, it feels like an incredibly real fear to you because you experienced it yourself for Jos, unsure if our relationship was worth fighting for in the face of this new, attractive, shiny thing full of potential but empty of lived experience and pain.

But even this lifelong fear isn’t the same any more, not for me. At the time, things were difficult for me because my emotions lagged behind my reason. I rationally wanted you to be free to do what you wanted, but also required the emotional protection of re-establishing control in case things went beyond my aspirational boundaries. I wanted you to be free, but felt the weight of that freedom as something that pushed me down. Now, my emotions have caught up with my reasoning. And while I know that this is personal growth to me, I also recognize that my growth is creating pain for you, and that's a serious problem. But… it also has some aspects I think are beneficial for us:

Before I found out about your betrayal, you already started putting Jos back on the table. When I found the book and you told me some little truths, when you asked to party with him again… these are actions that once would have triggered me and caused me to spiral in anxiety. But I was able to look at your reconnection genuinely not with jealousy but curiosity. That reaction drove something home for me. The message that upon revisiting these once traumatic emotions, they truly have matured and changed, from fear, jealousy, and control to just… acceptance. The betrayal complicated all that, but the lessons I had learned in the meantime were still true. I had grown. And now that I’ve had enough time for even that betrayal to settle, I am at peace with letting you live your life your way.

And Nathan - despite the initial conflation of your situation with him into the whole big mess, I think I proved something in how I handled it eventually, proving that my words about letting you have your freedom were more than just words.  I am not entitled to any of your life, I just want to be a good partner that you know you can talk to safely. And if you want to continue talking, it’s because I’ve earned anything you want to share through trust, rather than control.

Or the whole Dennis situation, again I felt mostly entertained and the desire to tease. And it feels good to be honest and in alignment with myself, even if it took some pain and work to get here.

—

Why you shouldn’t fear replacement

What makes me so confident that if I let my feelings grow, they won’t turn towards the expected monogamous script of wanting to replace you? You say that I’m unconventional, different, in so many ways. Can you extend a little bit of hope that in this way, and based off of my recent actions and feelings, I am different too?

You fulfil incredibly important things in my life - you are the exact type of companion that I want to laugh with, travel with, learn with, and live with. In fact, you took me when I was still young and moldable and created parts of me in your image - making us almost an impossible custom fit that no one else can replicate, including Melina or anyone else I might ever meet. I don’t just want you, I think I need you. But I’ve not also paused my own evolution from my own experiences, readings, and thoughts. And you haven’t grown in those same exact directions, much like I haven’t grown in all of yours. And that’s normal, to be expected. I will never stop valuing you for what you bring to me, but I can’t expect you to fulfil every aspect of me (though I did try), and it now feels unrealistic and unfair to expect that from you. For example, if you want to stain a bedsheet with many many stains that are yours alone, I don’t think I have it biologically in me to do that, soz. And if I want someone to discuss science with that doesn’t fall asleep to the sound of my voice, I don’t think you’re the right person either.

I can’t give you the guarantee that you’re always number 1 in all aspects of my life, nor do I think that comparison makes sense. But I can continue to value you for all of the things that you alone bring to my life, and help you feel more seen and valued in those respects. I’m sorry if I haven’t done a good job of doing that so far, and yes, it is a bit more difficult than usual to do it right now, when you need it most. That, unfortunately, just is what it is.

I want to know — what makes you feel special? I want you to feel as valued to me as you are. It’s just more difficult for me if the only thing that makes you feel special is “being above everyone else”.

Yes, we do have friends that we can put above others, like Tree over Phillson, so there are some types of hierarchy even in friendships. But in close friends, does that logic still make sense? Does Tree go above Tiara? Does one deserve more prioritizing than the other? There are things that you would even trust Tree with over me, or that she would trust you with over even Yan - and I don’t think there’s anything inherently wrong with that. I think every close person in our lives should be valued for exactly who they are - how they bring out the better parts of us, and we in them.

I don’t want to replace you. But neither do I want to repress myself into someone I am not to better fill your desires for what your partner should look like, rather than who they are.

My words can’t offer any guarantees that my actions over time can, so I'm hoping you can find a way to trust me. And I know I’m asking for trust on something that I haven’t shown trustworthiness on, that’s my fault. I’ve been selfish. I’ve prioritised what I wanted and progressed at the speed of what I wanted, not what you needed. And this upcoming trip must feel like a giant disregard for that. I understand how it looks and feels. And I feel almost powerless to argue for it in a way that doesn’t feel defensive, because I truly think you would have to be me to get it fully.

So… ok. I understand it’s going to take a lot more than just saying this to make you believe me.

But, a little more context:

Moving forward

Yes, we’ve been having our own problems recently, and that makes the moment feel particularly precarious. But the timing is just unfortunate, not a catalyst. I was already writing extensively to myself in unshared letters that I realize I've been accepting treatment I shouldn't. I’ve been sitting and thinking about it, and I realised something that will sound hurtful: for years, I've felt disrespected, made to feel small and incompetent. I have long-standing resentments I should have addressed earlier. They don't justify my actions, but I need to name them as we decide how to move forward.

In our much earlier years, there was subtle but persistent sexual coercion that still subconsciously affects how I feel about intimacy with you (and sex in general, with anyone, if I’m being fully honest). There was some disconnection when you met Jos and I started feeling like my dedication to our relationship wouldn’t always be reciprocal; that I really wasn’t an equal partner not just in the small things but in some of the big things also. There was fear when you told Joey at Ah Po’s funeral that divorce is not a big deal, and I couldn’t help but hear echoes of our unhappiness in the words and tone you used to describe others. I’ve been building resentment because I've asked you to get therapy for 2-3 years and you haven't followed through, even though you acknowledge the need. I’ve been building resentment because you seem not just unwilling but almost unable to put in the work that I require from you, not just the work you agree you want to do. I don’t think your inaction comes from a malicious place. I think it comes partially from not knowing what to do, partially from exhaustion due to work responsibilities, and partially from laziness where in your little free time you choose recreation over further emotional work. I understand this, and you know I make significant effort to prioritise your free time and your fun, but at this point I have to take a stronger stand.

I need to impose, because for too long my fear of imposing has started to feel more like self-betrayal.

And yet, despite all of this long and hurtful list of past and ongoing negativity... I still actively wanted and want to be with you, every single day. Because you are more than your faults, and what you add to my life clearly and easily overtakes what you cost it. I’m not saying that your faults are shallow. They will require substantial work to overcome. But I’m saying that my love is deeper still, and you are worth it. I see you working on it, and honestly it feels like a glimmer of hope I’d almost given up on. But I still need time to pass and see that this is a change of behavioural patterns, not a crisis-driven response.

There are things that we desperately need to work on together if we want things to improve in the longer-term. I don’t want to impose anything else, but I really think that doing the following things would give us the best path forward. I want us to both commit to regularly doing therapy for ourselves, starting within a month. I’d also like you to keep an open mind and finish ready the Ethical Slut at a reasonable pace so that we can start talking constructively from understanding rather than from pure emotion. I don’t need you to agree with everything the book says, just make a good faith effort at understanding it. And I need us to do couples therapy together also. Can we do that?

I know it’s a substantial amount of effort and money, but at this point, don’t we need it? If not now, then when??

I know I’m asking for a lot from you, so I will also do some concrete things to work on us.

- I’ll also do individual therapy, trying to help me get over my sexual issues, work on my patterns of dishonesty and my tendency towards conflict avoidance that usually just ends up ballooning problems (along with any other patterns you want me to discuss with the therapist and improve on).
- I’ll work to unpack any of my resentments to learn how to deal with them better.
- I’ll look into ways to deal with the erectile disfunction (doctor visit, maybe even pills, not just “hoping”).
- I’ll read any books that you want me to, alone or together, that might help us deal with our problems. I don’t want it to seem like you have to adapt to my way, I just want us to understand each other better.
- I’ll work on tempering my negative moods, and actively work on spending more quality time with you. I think we’ve been problem-focused for a little too long, it would be nice to find ways to be fun-focused again, and not just around sporadic vacations. These last few weeks with you, despite the problems we’re facing, feel more like a life I want to continue living with you, and I know that’s due mostly to your effort. In the future, I’ll take equal part in co-creating it.
- I will try to regularly remember all of the reasons I love you, and show you that you are still a very important part of my life, and a very special person that I have been chosen by.

—

So… what do I want? Definitely not to replace you. I value you. I love you. Not just for what we’ve been but for what we are, and what we still can be together. I love your sense of humour, the unexpected shit that comes out of your mouth that is so deeply incorrect sometimes yet so funny. I love seeing the little cute moments that reveal the intensity of your attachment to me, your vulnerability and concerns, and the trust you show me when making use of my intelligence (whether to proofread a report or listen for a fart). I love to see you push forward for your dreams, to see you deal with the considerable difficulties that life has thrown at you with resilience and courage. I love how strongly you react emotionally to the things that bring you joy, including of course, my actions. I love holding you, intertwining with you, feeling your skin against mine (especially when we are at different temperatures). I love our stupid little inside jokes, the dogboos, don’t-kiss-me-if-you-don’t-love-me moments, and 1-2-3 kisses that I know we will return to performing when things stabilise. Not just because they’re silly and freeing, but because they also speak to the lengths of comfort we’ve found within each other. I love, love, the fact that we still say good morning to each other every night (even if you’ve dropped the “kitty” ending for a while now). I love all the little signs of our daily compatibility- the way I know you just want to squat on Instagram in the corner of the room while I shower, that you’ll never say no to me asking for coffee, that you’ll understand my lying in bed with two floppy hands in the air means I want a hug. I love the way that even if we don’t always have time to talk about our problems, we don’t try to bury them forever.

And so many, countless other things that I can’t think of off the top of my head. I love you and I like you, and I want to keep you in my life for as long as you also want me there,
which I hope is until one of us dies (from old age ideally, so no need to touch wood).

I'm sorry I've been overly critical recently. I think emotional self-regulation, and other skills we can learn in therapy (separately or together) will help us navigate all of our issues better and so is not something to be delayed until “after”. But perhaps I’ve been too heavily critical in pushing for improvements or calling out the insufficiencies over showing appreciation for what we already have. I do appreciate you, truly. And I want to continue waking up next to you for the rest of my life (I will brush my teeth more and figure out something for the snoring so that this becomes more appealing to you too).

I know I’m asking a lot, but can we try? Can we find a way forward where we're both being fully honest about who we are and what we need, even when that's uncomfortable? I don't want to imply that my honesty depends on you accepting this - I owe you honesty regardless. For everything we’ve been and everything we still can be, I hope we’re willing to stick together and witness what this wonderful but chaotic journey of life has in store for us. We will change, but it will be beside each other. Like every time before, the problems are different but our willingness to stay and see things through proved stronger.

In short——

I’ve changed in a fundamental way. I want non-monogamy, including romantic connections. I know this isn't what you signed up for, and I know our relationship has existing issues I haven't fully addressed. I don’t think how I plan to live out my non-monogamy needs to be painful for you, though I also understand your massive and reasonable fear that as an unknown, it might get out of hand. I can do nothing but make the promise that you will always remain my priority. I know i’m asking you to change in a way you don’t want to, and I recognize that's deeply unfair. If you can't or don't want to, I'll accept that means we're no longer compatible, and I'll grieve that enormously. But I won't frame your decision as a failure to evolve or your pain as something you need to overcome. I'll just accept that I've grown in a direction that means we can't continue together, and that's a tragedy we're both facing, not a problem you need to fix.

Whatever your final decision, I’ll honour it. I want you to be the happiest you can be, with or without me. (Though of course I’m leaning towards “with”).

Besides, who else will wash your dishes and buy your chips? I’m not that easily replaceable either, based on my two biggest core strengths.

Yours, with all the weight that words entails,
SK.

entries.title: 15th Year
entries.note: re-reading this hurt. I remember thinking that things did not change substantially after it. The you, now, working on noise patterns for Quien, that you is who I wanted and needed at the time. But life got so much more complicated in between.
entries.body:
Hey kitty.

It’s our 15th anniversary today.

15 years is kind of nuts. When we first got together, you were a teenager. I was barely more than that, and in many ways due to my upbringing, very much still a teenager too.

I still remember John turning around and seeing us holding hands. I like how he didn’t say anything, but I wonder what he thought. It was a sweet moment.

I remember how, even years into the relationship, you’d comment on train rides that we must still look like a new couple to other people, because of how affectionate we were with each other.

I remember how hard we laughed when we got stoned for the first time in the US on that one-hitter. How your brain somehow went to the idea of my face looking like “honest Ken” and how thoroughly we lost it. It didn’t matter why we were laughing, just that we were.

Those were great times, but also more innocent ones.

Things were far from perfect, of course, and the problems of then were just as important as the problems of now, but perhaps just… simpler? 

I feel that as we got older and became more multifaceted and complicated people, the scope of our problems together also grew in complexity. We’ve both had to learn to grieve some of the things we expected from each other or for our futures.

The point is, life got complicated and we’ve both had to make sacrifices for each other, including ones we never thought we’d have to pay. We got complicated. And while yes, it does kind of suck, it’s also… reality. For all of our own intellectual knowledge that no one gets to live a perfect life, it feels different when you live that knowledge out. But it’s normal.

And it’s easy to lose perspective on the past, as well as the future. So I wanted to take today to remember the past a little, to celebrate some of our wins. Since this is my letter, let me share what I consider those to be.

First, thank you for seeing the potential in me. I absolutely was naive when you met me, a sheltered soul with a bunch of weird baggage I didn’t even know I was carrying. While I know my charming young looks did a number on you at first, you stayed because you realized there was more to me than that, and it’s that deeper layer that you fell in love with (I hope. Otherwise, getting older like this as a trophy guy, I’m fucked). You took that potential and you introduced me to your way of seeing the world. Some of which I still think is nonsense, sure, but a lot of which opened up my own ways of seeing and being.

I can look back at my life and see where it might have gone without you. I can see the distance between those possibilities and this reality. Even with all of our challenges, I am, by far, the version of me I’d rather be, and the difference between current me and possible thems isn’t small, but vast.

And that’s something I’ll owe you forever, no matter what happens, until my death. I think there’s something beautiful about being able to make a mark in someone’s life like that. Not just a specific moment, or even stretch, to be remembered, but a fundamental shift in how life is lived. You’ve helped me become, and be, me. (I’m not saying you did ALL the work, of course, I also did a lot of heavy lifting, so fuck you and suck my dick.)

You know, our wedding— that really still doesn’t mean anything much to me. Still just paper. But the strands of our lives are truly intertwined in the spirit of whatever weddings are supposed to represent. I love you, with the full depth of that word. I care about you, what happens to you, your safety, your health, your future, more than family (I know, not a ton coming from me), more than any other friend. It’s important to me that you live a great and fulfilling life, and it’s important that I help you achieve that.

I love you now, and can say with absolute certainty, that in many ways I always will.

But now we turn to the future. And while I would like this letter to be a moment of celebration, it’s a bit more complicated than that.

I still see the potential of us, in us, and want to see us make it through. But it’s precisely because I care that I need to be critical, that you will feel uncomfortable with some of the things I have to say next.

Our life doesn’t seem to be doing so poorly. We’re both content on the day to day. Work keeps us occupied, we spend our spare time together laughing together at shows and playing occasional computer games side-by-side. We fight less often than we used to. And when we do, we fight less furiously. These things can’t be discounted. We’ve grown, and achieved some major victories along the way that we don’t celebrate enough. But… our growth and ways of coping might also help hide issues underneath, smooth out our slow decline so it doesn’t look so rough from the outside. And by outside, I mean even when we, idly, choose to look at ourselves.

I think we’re having a very slow communication breakdown. Almost invisible in the day-to-day, but clear in the year-to-year. And communication is too important to ever be a casualty of time.

This isn’t the life I wanted for myself. I can’t imagine it’s the life you wanted either. We’ve reached a point of seeming stability, but I don’t want to look back at us and ever think “… is that all there is?” I’m learning it’s healthy to detach and follow my own interests, pursue my own growth, but still, I can’t help but feel… there’s got to be more to us than this.

I don’t know if you feel the same way, but I don’t feel like I can share all the honest or unfiltered thoughts with you. Somewhere along the way, I got used to the small white lie that keeps away the scary fight. The lie of “no” if you ask me if something I can tell you dislike turns me on. The lie of “yes” if I forgot to sanitize my hands after touching something small and inconsequentially “dirty”. None of these are important by themselves, but they’ve conditioned a state where it’s easier to keep the peace than to be myself with you, the one person I wish more than anything I could be myself with. I sometimes find myself rethinking actions or decisions based on your potential anger. It’s good, of course for me to think of you and be considerate of my partner, but this feels less like consideration and more like fear. Even here, now, I’m scared that these minor things I was just honest with you about will come back and haunt me, potentially for years. Maybe not spoken, but I’ll feel your eyes narrow when I touch something. I’ll feel you hold your tongue, but not your feelings.

I try as best as possible to accept the imperfect you, but it often feels like you hold me to a higher, more impossible standard. There’s little forgiveness, there’s “next time, don’t…” there’s no sighs and “it’s ok” there’s “why did you??”

I’m absolutely by no means perfect at being kind, but I’m exhausted by being the one who always extends the kindness while receiving a fraction in return. I’m tired of saying “it’s ok” to your fuckups while you reply “why are you so stupid” to mine. I’m by no means completely selfless but I’m exhausted by accommodating your little moments of selfishness.

I understand life stresses you more. Work drains you. Family disappoints you. Potential loss haunts you. You have so much more on your plate, and it is absolutely no fault of your own that you find yourself pressured. I don’t, and can’t, understand what that feels like. But I know it’s a heavy burden, and so I try to be patient and understanding as far as I can be.

I understand partners aren’t supposed to meet at 50/50, but share the load according to their capabilities. But how many years am I supposed to remain the 60 to 80? My whole life? I can’t sustain that.

How many more times must I clear absolute mental space to help you with your work, but feel tuned out as soon as I’m talking three sentences into something I care about that you don’t?

I’m hopeful that some of the pressures will lift, with time, but life goes on regardless, and I don’t want to live in hope of a timer that might never end, or even get worse. I can’t change life. Neither can you. But we can change how we respond to it. It’s not easy, but I sincerely believe it’s one of the most important skills we can master.

I have the luxury of time to work on myself. You don’t. I understand the desire to be relaxing when not working. But I think this work, emotional work, is in the long term more important than any job. For my sake, yes, but more so for your own.

I don’t think we can solve all of these things right away. I think they’re deeper problems, complex problems. And while I feel some relief in expressing them now, I know it would be stupid of me to expect things to change just because I’ve spoken up. 

But I don’t want this message to die as just another message. I don’t want this to just be me venting. I want it to go somewhere, help with something, flip that switch that makes the light of us, if not bright, at least increase in glow. I mean… 15 years. If not now, then when?

I want to be able to be fully myself around you. To feel more seen and invested in by you. And I want to see you more, too. The you that shows up full of life when we do stupid or fun stuff with our friends. The you I hear about in your dumbfuck interactions with Nathan. The creative, social you that’s hidden behind all the work. I miss the you that I’m sure you miss too.

And also… I want you to love me actively again, to be missed more than Diablo.

We’ve been together 15 years. That’s a real achievement, a lifetime sized achievement that no one can take away from us. It proves we have something special. Let’s not forget that it took hard work to get us here, and that we should put in the work to bring us further, and happier, for the next decade and more.

Right now, as a dish, life is a bit bittersweet. And I don’t trust our current skills in the kitchen. But I trust in us, if we care enough to learn.

entries.title: Closing 2025
entries.body:
Hey kitty,

I guess 2025 has been a pretty stressful year for you, seen through the lens of our relationship.

But I feel like I’ve talked enough about that for now, and we can have as many follow-up conversations as we need in person later. So instead, I just wanted to take the time to talk to you in the medium that is now most comfortable to me: semi-long-form letter :p

Life is weird, huh? And difficult. It rarely gives you what you want, and rarely when you want it or feel ready to receive it. Sometimes these things are bad, and sometimes they’re great. Oftentimes, they’re objectively neither– but just start to feel one way because of the prior expectations we hold about how things should be. Like... thinking something should go a certain way doesn't make it more likely to happen that way. It just creates this gap between expectation and reality that I have to grieve every time things don't line up. And that grief is optional, you know? For a long time I’ve still wanted you to be a slightly different version of yourself, someone that engages more with me in ways that I want rather than you are. But honestly, I’ve known you for long enough to recognise that you’re fundamentally someone who doesn’t do well with significant change, you’re fiercely independent and stubborn in all the best (and worst) ways. I like and love who you are, but I think I’ve been focusing too much on who you could be. Constantly looking at that gap of course starts to feel draining. All I see is the empty space between the person you are and the imaginary outline that I wished you could be. The relationship we actually have is different from what I imagined, but that doesn't make it wrong… just real. And I’m sure I’m different from the idea you have of who I could be also: someone more organised, more proactive, more sexual. And those are things you might also have to grieve. But they’re also what makes me real.

That’s not to say I don’t want to become more the person you want me to be, nor put in the effort. That’s the incredible thing about love: it pushes us to be better people for someone else, in ways that make us better people even for ourselves. And I hope you’re also open to changing in ways that move towards my ideal too. But without expectation, without punishment for future failures, that if either one of us come short it doesn’t matter. It doesn’t matter as long as we’re still trying.

I’m really glad you found Nathan. I can tell that he adds a lot to your life in ways that I don’t currently (and most likely ever can). And I’m glad I met Melina. She does so similarly for me. I know it’s not the same situation on both sides, but that was never the point. I’m not trying to give you more of what I want for myself to lessen the guilt. I just want you to live as full a life as you can, while I similarly try to live mine. I know my life philosophy isn’t compatible with yours in some pretty destabilising ways, and I’m sorry about that. But that’s just who I am now, and I don’t want to continue living having to hide that from you. I deserve better than that, so do you, and so does the relationship that we’ve worked so hard to build together over the last nearly-16 years. And just as I'm asking you to see me as I am now, I want to understand who you're becoming. What this has meant for you, what's changed in your inner world, what you're discovering about the life you want.

I hope that in retrospect you’ve valued (or will eventually value) this time apart that we’ve had. I hope that when I come back I’ll be able to meet you again, not as who I’ve been but as who I am now. And I trust that you’ll meet me with the same unapologetic honesty – which should be easier for you, because that’s closer to who you’ve always been than I.

So, yes, 2025 was complicated, but I also think, necessary. And no matter what the future brings, you will always have been a huge part of my life, and a huge one moving forward. I’ll fight for that, no matter what shape it takes. You’re still my partner and best friend, and I don’t take those things lightly. I hope 2026 lets us have many difficult conversations that bring us closer to what we originally were: just two teenagers who saw something incredible and attractive in the other, and decided to walk that path and see wherever it leads. But also towards something we’ve never been: two adults who have earned each other’s trust through repeated failures and clear communication, even when we're becoming people our younger selves wouldn't recognize. Even as two people who continue choosing each other while also choosing ourselves.

With deep love,

Your kitty.

entries.title: 14th Year
entries.note: still can't believe you beat me at Divinity. Also, fuck Bairdotr.
entries.body:
Hey kitty,

I’m honestly a bit sick of your “Can you believe it’s been x time since” type questions. You know I’m not one to dwell on the past very much. Although when I do indulge, usually in the middle of some cleaning or digital purge, I get sucked in just as powerfully as you, reminisce just as hard, and possibly even beat you on nostalgia.

A lot of little things get lost to time, little words, little actions, little “jiups!” as you raise an invisible cup to scoop the little stars of my shoulders, little exclamations like “huggles!” as we acted like ill-fitting, slippery bodies on top of each other. To make ourselves feel better, we reflect that they no longer make us feel quite the same way, and we call it “growth” or “maturity”.

But it’s not, really, is it? Because if you put your lips against my cheek and I let out a stupid little “chuuu”, there’s still something unexplainably adorable about it. Or if you hug me and put your bare feet on top of mine, and I walk around the room with you, even walking across the room becomes a little adventure. And as much as you insist it’s fucking stupid, you’ll still swing your hand next to mine, finger extended, until I catch it with a dumb sound effect.

We’re not maturing at all, not when it comes to this. We’re just filing away our old favourites and creating new songs to vibe to, not wishing to forget, but content to revisit.

And we have so much to revisit: we’ve been to 10 countries together, went to (and came in) US national parks with car-sized trees, froze our socks off at a Swiss lake, ate at a bedouin’s house in the Atlas mountains…  but we also got betrayed by a trusted friend before you eventually proved yourself the stronger god (not better, just stronger). We travelled from planet to planet and deep in mines, and built the dumbest-named farm together with demonic-inspired chickens.

We’ve hugged. So very much. Traveling without traveling at all, safety and comfort in stillness and warmth. Little moments that accumulate in their absence (rare) and their presence (plentiful).

So, “Can you believe it’s been 14 years since we got together?”

Happy 14th Anniversary, kitty.

entries.title: 12th Year
entries.body:
Hey kitty… I’m finding it really difficult to write this letter. I haven’t been procrastinating just because I have work or other commitments - each of the three previous times I sat down to write, nothing much came out. I aborted one attempt after multiple failed starts. Aborted another after the tone of the letter naturally turned sour. Aborted the latest attempt just now because I wanted to write about what’s really on my mind, as potentially unpleasant as some of it might be.

The purpose of the anniversary letter is to celebrate the relationship. But I’m finding it a little bit hard to feel in a celebratory mood. The last few months have been tough on me. I’ve done my best to be open and vulnerable about my feelings, but I feel like my actions are being met more and more with impatience, with frustration, with delaying tactics, with often repeated and hollow-sounding promises. Now when I think of talking to you again, the only thoughts that run through my head are “has it been long enough since the last time? How will you take it? Will this just make you upset and push you away even more?”. I don’t like fearing you. I don’t need therapy to tell me that it’s an extremely unhealthy relational dynamic.

I wouldn’t want to memorialise in an anniversary letter any minor concerns or annoyances that I’m feeling in the moment. But I feel like that’s where we’re at. That for me to feel properly heard, I have to do something as drastic as this. So, I’m sorry, but this letter will be more bitter than sweet. I think it’s what we need right now. To clear the lines of communication. To be real.

So, here goes everything.

You must be so tired of hearing me talk about the imbalance in our power dynamic by now. I know it can be tempting to just sort of shut off and think “not this thing again” when it pops up, but it’s been popping up with increasing frequency and weight because it’s something I’m genuinely struggling with and hurt over. And it doesn’t seem to be moving in a direction of resolution. It felt like a small victory a few weeks ago when I asked you if you felt the power imbalance between us and you admitted that you did. And that’s great. I’m glad to hear of your self-awareness on this issue. But self-awareness without action leaves me in exactly the same situation that I started in.

I brought up simple examples of things you could start doing to show me you were committed to working on the problem: reading wait but why, working on the tattoo drawing, reading Homo Deus, writing that note with your perspective on our open status… those alone wouldn’t fix the power imbalance, but they would show me that you are willing to commit to clearly identified tasks that you know would help. I’ve given you time and space for you to initiate action. When nothing materialised, I tried a more active approach. On Jan 20, I told you before going to sleep that I knew you already had a lot on your plate, but that it would mean a lot to me if you followed through on some of the commitments you’ve promised me. To be very honest, at that point I was already not confident that what I said would make any difference. I prefaced my encouragement to you as gently and kindly as I could. I started with “it would mean a lot to me if you did this…” 

It’s been over three weeks. Having no follow up to that whatsoever says something quite important about the state of our relationship, whether you want to see it that way or not. It’s been over three weeks. What have you done? Have you even thought about it? If you have, it would really help if you at least tell me that you’re thinking about it, because from my perspective it just looks like another empty promise. You push me to take actions to help keep me on track like set reminders and make plans - where are your reminders? Where are your plans? Why should you be allowed to get upset at me not staying true to my word when your violations are so much more egregious? I’ve literally been waiting for years for you to take action. I’ve tried to entice you. I’ve gotten angry at you. I’ve been kind. I’ve been supportive. I’ve been harsh. I’ve given you time. All of these. Multiple times. Nothing. And not even a hyperbolic nothing, an actual, literal nothing.

If the power imbalance between us remains conceptual to you, this is what it looks like. Laid out as plainly as our history shows it to be. An open wound that has been festering for years, that you could start working to close with a single hour’s commitment. Yet I’ve spent more time chasing you to abide by your word than you have spent honouring it. And these things I’m chasing you to do are supposedly things that you already want to do. How much less influence do you think I would have if I tried pushing you towards things you find naturally unpleasant? Are we in this relationship as mutually respected partners, or am I just along for the ride for as long as I give you what you want? It’s not that I don’t recognise that you do amazing things for me. You’ve given me the best memories of my life. You’ve organised amazing surprise birthday parties for which you worked tirelessly hours upon hours. You’ve drawn amazing images and created beautiful objects of great sentimentality and workmanship. Without discounting any of those things, I still need to point out that none of those were things I wanted you to do for me. They were all things you wanted to do for me.

I’ve sacrificed so much for you. Rearranged my expectations and my boundaries to give you greater pleasure in recent experiences with Jos. I’ve suffered for your happiness. Let me be more precise, I’ve willingly chosen to suffer through unpleasantness, so that I could normalise the pain and give you what you wanted. That’s sacrifice. That’s effort. That’s love.

How much do I ask for from you? How much have you had to change your life for me to fit within it? Yes, you’ve always been a rock and I’ve molded myself around you, but am I slowly coming to understand that you are not unwilling but incapable of change for me?

I looked up the definition of resentment in the dictionary yesterday to see if it applied to my current feelings.

“Resentment is a complex, multilayered emotion that has been described as a mixture of disappointment, disgust, anger, and fear.”

No disgust, but the others… Yes to disappointment, to hearing you repeatedly fail to keep to your simple commitments. Yes to anger, built over days, weeks, and months of not having my few, rare desires met. And yes to fear, the fear to even broach this topic with you in the face of your decreasing patience. I’m disappointed, angry, and too scared to talk about it. I don’t want to even hear myself writing these words, but the description is accurate. I’m starting to resent you over this.

Relationships don’t need to be perfectly symmetrical in every way. I can be more of a giver than you. But it’s non-negotiable that there needs to be mutual respect.

Of course it’s not all bad. Our relationship isn’t in actual danger. I still love you deeply, and I know you do the same for me. We’re still at a place that we should be proud of, we’re still the best relationship I know. We still know how to create our own fun, we’ve still got each other’s backs, I still admire you and you still admire me. I still find you hot. You still find me cool (jokes). We’re still the best communicators we know. I just want to live up to our historical standard. I want things to feel like the way they used to.

But the thing is, things were never this way. But if we’re going to be happy in the long run, that will have to change. I sacrifice and compromise because you deserve that from your equal, your partner. Don’t I deserve that too?

Sorry for hijacking our 12th year note for this. But it belongs in our history, and I hope it leads to an amazing 13th one next year.

entries.title: 11th Year
entries.note: our love will always be our cat
entries.body:
If our love were a cat, with 11 years of life in it, it would be a lazy beast. It would be comfortable in its dominion of the house, most of its restless energy gone. It would drape the couch, a familiar puddle of fur in a familiar favourite spot.

But our love is not lazy. It is comfortable in its dominion of our minds, only because it no longer needs to prove itself over and over. Our love is not a demand but a choice- one we reveal in every little self-sacrificing action; our life put on hold for the greater purpose of serving another we see as deserving of love and respect.

Our love lacks restless energy because that energy is purposefully channeled. Every touch is intentioned. The words “I love you” may not punctuate the end of many sentences or even be heard for days, but when they are spoken, they are spoken fiercely. They reveal the brightly burning embers under the coal dust of everyday regularity. They do not ignite a fire in us only because the fire has never stopped burning.

Our love drapes the couch, fur glinting in the afternoon sun. When we stop moving, it is not because we lack the energy to run. It is because we have found peace in the quietness that is provided by our moments of dual solitude. We are inseparable, alone when we are together. And we contain inner worlds rich enough to live out our whole lives. The outer world is auxiliary.

If our love was a cat, it would be loved by our friends. Some will have seen its birth. Most will have seen its growth. Many may think they understand it.

Silly humans. They have no idea.

entries.title: The proposal
entries.note: this will always be one of the happiest moments of my life
entries.body:
I’ve always been a bit of a romantic, so while I never spent much time thinking about the future, I did occasionally look forward to proposing and getting married. And for much of my youth, it was a simple but consistent vision: the proposal would be some combination of kneeling to ask the question / a ring / celebrating with friends / some elaborately planned surprise. Fast forward to the present, and I did it lying down, without a ring, with no friends present. The only thing I got ‘right’ was that it was a surprise - but it was a bit of a surprise to me as well as to you.

If it all works out the way you want it to, proposals are something you only get to do this once in your life. Yet what I eventually did was nothing at all like what I had, for years, wanted it and thought it would be. So, by most accounts, in terms of meeting expectations, it could be described as a failure.

But it was the most beautiful and meaningful failure I ever could have asked for.

The only thing I failed at was to live up to my younger self’s desires. But that younger self isn’t me. The only expectations I betrayed were those of a different life - of the person I was before I became the person I am. That person could have gone to law school and might already have children. That person bought jewelry for his future partner in some ridiculously romantic (sorry, ridiculous and romantic) gesture. I don’t want to be that person. I want to be this person, the one who discovered himself with you by my side, this intellectual but silly, charming but corny, imperfect person who’s made perfectly happy with you.

And for this person, an unexpected proposal might not have been so unexpected.
